recommendations.title: Heroin(e)
recommendations.overview: This documentary follows three women — a fire chief, a judge, and a street missionary — as they battle West Virginia's devastating opioid epidemic.

recommendations.title: The Night of the Hunter
recommendations.overview: In Depression-era West Virginia, a serial-killing preacher hunts two young children who know the whereabouts of a stash of money.

recommendations.title: October Sky
recommendations.overview: The true story of Homer Hickam, a coal miner's son who was inspired by the first Sputnik launch to take up rocketry against his father's wishes.

recommendations.title: Winter on Fire: Ukraine's Fight for Freedom
recommendations.overview: Over 93 days in Ukraine, what started as peaceful student demonstrations became a violent revolution and full-fledged civil rights movement.

recommendations.title: Crip Camp: A Disability Revolution
recommendations.overview: Down the road from Woodstock in the early 1970s, a revolution blossomed in a ramshackle summer camp for disabled teenagers, transforming their young lives and igniting a landmark movement.

recommendations.title: Money Heist: The Phenomenon
recommendations.overview: A documentary on why 'Money Heist' sparked a wave of enthusiasm around the world for a lovable group of thieves and their professor.

recommendations.title: Becoming
recommendations.overview: Join former first lady Michelle Obama in an intimate documentary looking at her life, hopes and connection with others.

recommendations.title: Matewan
recommendations.overview: Filmed in the coal country of West Virginia, "Matewan" celebrates labor organizing in the context of a 1920s work stoppage. Union organizer, Joe Kenehan, a scab named "Few Clothes" Johnson and a sympathetic mayor and police chief heroically fight the

recommendations.title: Holy Hell
recommendations.overview: An inside look at a West Hollywood cult formed by a charismatic teacher in the 1980s that eventually imploded.

recommendations.title: Baraka
recommendations.overview: A paralysingly beautiful documentary with a global vision—an odyssey through landscape and time—that attempts to capture the essence of life.
